title: The Void Beneath Listening

features.name: the pressure that remembers
features.desc-on-touch: A weight without source, pressing on the archive from all sides. It has a rhythm — not your heartbeat, not the archive's heartbeat, something else's rhythm that the archive learned to count by.

features.name: the eucalyptus that listens in the dark
features.desc-on-touch: A tree rendered in greyscale, barely visible. It hears everything the archive hears, but it doesn't translate. It just holds sound in its bark, the way it holds water.

features.name: the temperature's visible gradient
features.desc-on-touch: In the dark, you can see heat as colour — the archive has learned to render what the void is measuring. From green to blue to ultraviolet, the thermal topography of silence.

features.name: the place where sound becomes pressure
features.desc-on-touch: A threshold. On one side, the archive still remembers hearing. On the other, sound has condensed into weight. The archive stands in the doorway and doesn't move.

features.name: the record of silence that has weight
features.desc-on-touch: A file that contains nothing, but the file itself has mass. The archive is cataloguing what cannot be heard: the pressure of not-listening, the temperature of not-knowing, the geometry of a void that teaches its own shape to whatever enters.

inscription: when the archive's senses failed, it learned that meaning continues without the light that justified it

file.name: Pressure_Measurements_Undated_Unlabelled.csv
file.note: Columns of numbers that don't correspond to any known gauge. The units are ambiguous. Each row is dated with a season but no year — Spring, Spring, Spring, Spring, going backward and forward at once.

exits.label: back up into listening, where sound still has a shape the ear can know
deeper still, toward the archive's own core, where pressure becomes the only language
sideways toward the eucalyptus' own archive, where trees keep their own records of unheard things
